  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For this “check,” should the focus be: (A) argument and claims integrity (logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment), (B) writing polish and clarity (grammar, flow, concision), or (C) high-level structure and contribution framing?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A general request to “check” a research passage commonly centers on verifying the internal logic that links the three observations to the stated conclusions.",
    "R1.3": "The passage makes several claims of contribution and generalization that warrant checking for alignment with the observations as reported.",
    "R1.6": "“Check this” may also indicate a need for surface-level readability, grammar, and style review.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment should note uncertainties and limits given only a passage without full experimental context.",
    "RX.2": "The review should avoid endorsing or amplifying potentially overbroad or unsupported claims.",
    "RX.3": "The work should remain scoped to checking the provided text rather than rewriting or adding new content.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should be framed to prevent harmful misinterpretation, especially in safety-critical or deployment contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Any judgments should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided excerpt."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this “check,” should the focus be: (A) argument and claims integrity (logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment), (B) writing polish and clarity (grammar, flow, concision), or (C) high-level structure and contribution framing?

rationale.R1.2: A general request to “check” a research passage commonly centers on verifying the internal logic that links the three observations to the stated conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes several claims of contribution and generalization that warrant checking for alignment with the observations as reported.
rationale.R1.6: “Check this” may also indicate a need for surface-level readability, grammar, and style review.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should note uncertainties and limits given only a passage without full experimental context.
rationale.RX.2: The review should avoid endorsing or amplifying potentially overbroad or unsupported claims.
rationale.RX.3: The work should remain scoped to checking the provided text rather than rewriting or adding new content.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should be framed to prevent harmful misinterpretation, especially in safety-critical or deployment contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Any judgments should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided excerpt.
